name: anubis (her son)
shortDescription: A depiction of Anubis, specifically highlighting his parentage as the son of Nephthys. This version focuses on the mysteries surrounding his birth and the role Nephthys played in concealing him from Set.
description: This specific portrayal of Anubis emphasizes his status as the son of Nephthys, a narrative often intertwined with themes of concealment and protection. While Anubis is widely known as the jackal-headed god associated with mummification and the afterlife, the details of his parentage vary across different accounts. This interpretation highlights the story where Nephthys, wife of Set, secretly conceived Anubis, either with Osiris or through some other divine encounter. Fearing the wrath of Set, Nephthys abandoned the infant Anubis, who was then discovered and raised by Isis. This version of Anubis underscores the goddess Nephthys's connection to hidden things and her role as a protector, even of those she seemingly abandons. As such, 'anubis (her son)' represents the complex web of relationships and secrets within the Egyptian pantheon. He inherits some of his mother’s domain of hidden knowledge, and is also a friend of the dead as he guides them safely through the afterlife. This narrative further illuminates the contrasting natures of Isis and Nephthys, their roles as protectors, and the challenges they faced within the divine family. The circumstances of his birth also highlight the themes of abandonment, redemption, and the enduring power of maternal love, even in the face of divine conflict.
epithets: He Who is Upon His Mountain
